question.text: National Savings Certificates (NSC VIII issue) have a tenor of 5 years with interest compounded annually. Which of the following entities is NOT eligible to purchase NSCs?

answer.text: Resident individual on behalf of a minor
Two joint holders on either-or-survivor basis

explanation.text: **The Logic:** NRIs, HUFs, Companies, trusts, societies, and other institutions are not allowed to purchase NSCs. Only resident individuals can buy — either on their own account, on behalf of minors, or jointly with up to two joint holders. **The Trap:** Joint holding by two individuals is allowed, so this might seem like a restriction but isn't. **Cross-Exam Context:** Small saving scheme eligibility is tested in X-A and relevant for tax planning in V-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** NSC's accrued interest is taxable but deemed reinvested — making it eligible for Section 80C benefit year after year. This is a unique feature not available in most other instruments.
